meta.title: Driver Fatigue Management — Night-Trunk Fleet, Route R-204
meta.subtitle: Hours-of-service compliance + advisory fatigue index (FMCSA 49 CFR 395 / EU 561/2006)
meta.author: HSE Logistics & Transport
meta.deid_notice: All drivers are referred to by role label. The driver's medical-fitness / OSA detail is held in a separate confidential occupational-health record; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: Night-trunk fleet, route R-204, Midlands depot — single-shift duty log reviewed against the EU 561/2006 drivers' hours (the UK GB-domestic operation is read with EU 561). The hours-of-service compliance flags are COMPUTED by the fatigue.py engine from the supplied duty log, not narrated. The fatigue index below is a clearly-flagged ADVISORY metric, never a regulatory threshold.

Compliance-flags-primary: the per-rule PASS/FAIL flags below are the authoritative output, computed deterministically by fatigue.py (eu561_compliance / fmcsa_compliance) from the duty log. The fatigue index in the next section is advisory only.

sections.caption: EU Regulation (EC) 561/2006 per-rule compliance — computed by fatigue.py (numbers are LOCKED engine constants; weekly/fortnightly need the multi-day log)

sections.text: One or more hours-of-service rules are breached: the 45-min break after 4.5 h continuous driving (Art. 7) was not taken. Reschedule the shift to insert the qualifying break. The fatigue index is advisory only; the compliance flags are the authoritative finding.
Advisory fatigue index (ADVISORY / derived — NOT a regulatory threshold)

ADVISORY / derived metric — a transparent weighted-stressor heuristic (0.4 driving load + 0.3 time-awake + 0.3 circadian timing, peaking in the Window of Circadian Low ~02:00-06:00), NOT a regulatory threshold and NOT a validated biomathematical model. Use the EU 561 / FMCSA compliance flags above as the authoritative output.

items: [GAP] FMCSA 60/70 h cycle & 34 h restart: not assessable from a single-shift log — collect the 7-/8-day cumulative on-duty figure (the engine defaults these to compliant only because no multi-day breach is detectable, not because compliance is proven).
[GAP] EU 561 weekly (56 h) / fortnightly (90 h) driving: not supplied — request the multi-day tachograph download. Never asserted compliant from one shift.

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (roster redesign first, alertness last)

rows.control: Redesign the R-204 roster: split the trunk run into a two-driver relay so the qualifying 45-min break and the 11 h daily rest are built into the schedule (FRMS)
Journey-plan the route to place the rest stop before the 4.5 h continuous-driving point
In-cab fatigue-detection alerting as an engineered BACKSTOP on the night leg (not the headline control)
Fatigue-risk rostering rules + a short driver fatigue briefing as administrative reinforcement

sections.text: A 'stay alert' briefing or an in-cab fatigue-detection gadget is NOT the primary control. A treatment that leads with driver-alertness training on a roster that forces a break breach is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — fatigue is led by roster / journey-plan / built-in-rest (FRMS) redesign.

rows.action: Reschedule R-204 to a two-driver relay so the 45-min break is built into the shift
Collect the 7/8-day cumulative on-duty figure and the weekly/fortnightly driving totals to close the multi-day [GAP]s
Pilot in-cab fatigue detection on the night leg as a backstop

items: The duty log was supplied by the operator (ELD/tachograph) — a missing segment would be a [GAP], never an invented driving hour or rest segment.
Multi-day cycle / restart / weekly-driving figures not supplied → [GAP] (see above); never asserted compliant from a single shift.
India site (if applicable): state return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
EU Regulation (EC) 561/2006 — drivers' hours (9-10 h daily driving / 45-min break after 4.5 h / 11 h daily rest / 56 h weekly / 90 h fortnightly); Reg (EU) 165/2014 tachograph
FMCSA Hours-of-Service — 49 CFR Part 395 (11 h driving / 14 h window / 30-min break / 60-70 h cycle / 34 h restart) where the operation is US
Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS) principles — schedule/roster redesign first; India Motor Transport Workers Act 1961 / state rules via hse-india
Review trigger: on roster change / on ELD-exception / quarterly — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person must review before use.
